what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel that comments on board://item-02d8b53b and endorses a paragraph arguing UI comments should anchor to stable ui:// identities rather than screen coordinates.
observations: YAML frontmatter records id, board:// address, type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to item-02d8b53b.
Body quotes a paragraph about Figma's limitation of comments not following nested elements because Figma anchors to coordinates/top-frame.
It asserts the system already tags elements with data-ui-ref → ui://, a stable semantic identity independent of position.
It warns against anchoring UI comments to coordinates, calling that a deliberate recreation of Figma's worst bug.
The note ends with the brief informal endorsement 'Yes, banger.'
standouts: Endorsement of semantic ui:// identity as the anchor for UI-mode comments.
Explicit warning that coordinate anchoring would recreate Figma's long-standing nested-comment bug.
Links to a prior board item via commented_on.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML metadata schema with id, address, type, source, state, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses board:// URI addressing and ISO 8601 timestamps.
Uses a markdown body with a [paragraph] tag and a short reaction.
concerns: The body is mostly an endorsement with little added reasoning of its own.
'Yes, banger.' is informal and may not carry actionable detail for future readers.
The full argument depends on the linked item-02d8b53b, which is not included here.
notes: Tim's short endorsement note agreeing that UI comments must anchor to stable ui:// refs rather than coordinates, referencing an earlier board argument.
questions: What is the full text and context of the referenced item-02d8b53b?
What does 'UI-mode comments' mean in concrete implementation terms?
Is data-ui-ref already implemented or still proposed?
Are there specific cases where coordinate anchoring is still acceptable (e.g., phone UI)?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a note/comment; actual content is an endorsement of a prior design argument — they match.
apparent_themes: Stable element references in UI systems
intention_signals: To record agreement with and amplify the principle that UI comments should be anchored to stable element identity (ui://) rather than transient screen coordinates.
novelty: Frames the system's existing data-ui_ref/ui:// mechanism as the direct answer to Figma's famously unresolved nested-comment limitation.
contribution: Preserves a concise design principle and a warning about coordinate-based comment anchoring, along with a link to the originating argument, so a fused system retains the rationale for identity-based anchoring.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim endorsing a prior argument that UI comments should anchor to stable data-ui-ref/ui:// identities rather than screen coordinates to avoid Figma's nested-element comment bug.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement with and reinforce the principle of semantic identity-based anchoring for UI comments.
